_meta.editorial_review_notes:
Provenance notes for this specimen.
AI-drafted fields are marked with `ai_drafted: true` in each row's field_provenance block.

AI-drafted field counts in this specimen:
  - 15 × markets.underlying_reference
  - 1 × events.editorial_notes
  - 1 × events.tags

Provenance rules:
  - Kalshi `resolution_source_name` and `resolution_source_url` are always pulled from the series `settlement_sources` API field.
  - Polymarket `resolution_source_name` uses an editorial default ('Credible news reporting — subjective') only when the platform API ships the field empty.
  - `underlying_reference`: per-market AI draft, anchored on market close_at for authoritative dates.
  - `editorial_notes`: per-event AI draft, 2-3 sentences of institutional framing.
  - `tags`: 4-6 per event, mix of thematic, attribute, and entity tags, anchored on the authoritative year from close_at.
  - `question`: AI rewrite of raw platform prose into canonical grammar when needed; otherwise platform value retained.

events.question: What will the Federal Reserve announce at the April 2026 FOMC meeting?
events.editorial_notes: These markets track the Federal Reserve Board of Governors' policy rate decision at the April 29, 2026 FOMC meeting. Polymarket prices directional outcomes via its UMA Optimistic Oracle: cut 50+ bps, cut 25 bps, hold, or hike. Kalshi prices absolute rate level via Kalshi staff determination, using binary strikes from 2.75% to 5.25% on the upper bound. Both platforms resolve against the Federal Reserve Board of Governors' official target range announcement.

markets.question_raw: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the April 2026 meeting?
markets.description_raw:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.
markets.underlying_reference: The Federal Reserve Board of Governors' announcement of the upper bound of the target federal funds rate following the April 2026 FOMC meeting.
markets.resolution_rules_raw:

markets.resolution_source_name: Federal Reserve (per market description; UMA arbitrates).

markets.question_raw: Will the upper bound of the federal funds rate be above 3.25% following the Fed's Apr 29, 2026 meeting?
markets.underlying_reference: The Federal Reserve Board of Governors' announcement of the federal funds rate target range following the April 29, 2026 Federal Open Market Committee meeting.
markets.resolution_rules_raw:
If the upper bound of the target federal funds rate published on the Federal Reserve's official website is greater than 3.25% following the Federal Reserve's Apr 29, 2026 meeting, then the market resolves to Yes.

This market will expire the first 2:05 PM ET following the release of a Federal Reserve statement for their Apr 29, 2026 meeting or one week following the last day of that meeting.
